title: tragopogon orientalis subsp. leiocarpos
intro: Tragopogon orientalis subsp. leiocarpos, also known as the Oriental Salsify or Eastern Salsify, is a subspecies of the Tragopogon orientalis plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been shown to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The roots of the plant can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions. Additionally, the plant is also used as a food source, with its edible roots and shoots being used in salads and other dishes, particularly in Eastern European and Middle Eastern cuisine.
